course: XSS Defense & Secure Data Parsing Lab

lessons.title: Reflected XSS and the Sink Map
topics: Trace untrusted input from request to DOM sink
Reproduce a reflected XSS, then neutralize it with `textContent` and contextual output handling
Build a reusable map of dangerous DOM sinks to watch for
objects: Object: Lesson: Reflected XSS and the Sink Map
Assessment: Quiz: Reflected XSS and the Sink Map

lessons.title: Stored XSS and Safe Rendering
topics: How persisted, attacker-controlled data executes on render
Render stored data without executing it
Encode-on-output discipline across DOM updates
objects: Object: Lesson: Stored XSS and Safe Rendering
Assessment: Quiz: Stored XSS and Safe Rendering

lessons.title: DOM-Based XSS — innerHTML vs. textContent
topics: Identify client-side-only XSS that never reaches the server
Refactor `innerHTML` / `insertAdjacentHTML` sinks to `textContent` and safe DOM APIs
When `createElement` + `append` beats string-built HTML
objects: Object: Lesson: DOM-Based XSS — innerHTML vs. textContent
Assessment: Quiz: DOM-Based XSS — innerHTML vs. textContent

lessons.title: Dangerous Contexts — URLs, Attributes, and Event Handlers
topics: `javascript:` URLs and unsafe `href` / `src` assignment
Attribute injection and `setAttribute` pitfalls
Inline event handlers (`on*`) as XSS sinks and their safe alternatives
objects: Object: Lesson: Dangerous Contexts — URLs, Attributes, and Event Handlers
Assessment: Quiz: Dangerous Contexts — URLs, Attributes, and Event Handlers

lessons.title: Rendering Untrusted HTML Safely
topics: When HTML must be rendered: sanitization as XSS defense
Using a vetted sanitizer (DOMPurify) and a manual allow-list approach
Verifying the sanitized output actually blocks the payload
objects: Object: Lesson: Rendering Untrusted HTML Safely
Assessment: Quiz: Rendering Untrusted HTML Safely

lessons.title: Defensive JSON.parse on Untrusted Input
topics: Never trust the shape: validate parsed JSON against an expected schema
Handle malformed, partial, and hostile payloads without crashing
Type and bounds checks before using parsed values
objects: Object: Lesson: Defensive JSON.parse on Untrusted Input
Assessment: Quiz: Defensive JSON.parse on Untrusted Input

lessons.title: Prototype Pollution via Insecure Object Handling
topics: How `__proto__` / `constructor` keys poison objects during merge or assign
Reproduce a prototype-pollution attack sourced from parsed JSON
Defend with null-prototype objects, key guards, and safe merge
objects: Object: Lesson: Prototype Pollution via Insecure Object Handling
Assessment: Quiz: Prototype Pollution via Insecure Object Handling

lessons.title: JSON.stringify Pitfalls and Safe Serialization
topics: Accidental data leakage through over-broad serialization
`replacer` functions and `toJSON` to control what is emitted
`reviver` functions on parse; circular-reference and type hazards
objects: Object: Lesson: JSON.stringify Pitfalls and Safe Serialization
Assessment: Quiz: JSON.stringify Pitfalls and Safe Serialization

lessons.title: Insecure Object Handling Patterns
topics: Unsafe dynamic property access and client-side mass assignment
Type coercion and equality bugs that bypass checks
Safe object-handling patterns for untrusted data
objects: Object: Lesson: Insecure Object Handling Patterns
Assessment: Quiz: Insecure Object Handling Patterns

lessons.title: Secure Render Pipeline — fetch → parse → render
topics: Consume an API, parse the response defensively, and render to the DOM safely
Place each control at the right layer of the pipeline
Verify no untrusted value reaches a dangerous sink
objects: Object: Lesson: Secure Render Pipeline — fetch → parse → render
Assessment: Quiz: Secure Render Pipeline — fetch → parse → render

lessons.title: Find-and-Fix Audit Lab
topics: Audit a small vulnerable client app for XSS and unsafe-parsing flaws
Prioritize and remediate each finding
Confirm fixes with the attack payloads that previously succeeded
objects: Object: Lesson: Find-and-Fix Audit Lab
Assessment: Quiz: Find-and-Fix Audit Lab

lessons.title: Defense-in-Depth Scenario Lab
topics: Harden a realistic feature that combines rendering and data parsing
Layer safe DOM APIs, sanitization, schema validation, and safe object handling
Demonstrate the feature resists a scripted set of attacks
objects: Object: Lesson: Defense-in-Depth Scenario Lab
Assessment: Quiz: Defense-in-Depth Scenario Lab
